title: [VectorCombine] Optimize vector combine in fold binop of reduction
author: Anjian-Wen
description: move the reduction op close by simple commutative property of Addition and Associative Property of Subtraction, which can pave the way for combining the next two instructions in some case and saving a reduction instruction if possible.

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i8:8:32-i16:16:32-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128-Fn32"
target triple = "aarch64"

; Function Attrs: nounwind uwtable vscale_range(1,16)
define dso_local i32 @test(ptr noundef %p1, i32 noundef %s_p1, ptr noundef %p2, i32 noundef %s_p2) #0 {
entry:
  %p1.addr = alloca ptr, align 8
  %s_p1.addr = alloca i32, align 4
  %p2.addr = alloca ptr, align 8
  %s_p2.addr = alloca i32, align 4
  %i_sum = alloca i32, align 4
  %y = alloca i32, align 4
  %cleanup.dest.slot = alloca i32, align 4
  %x = alloca i32, align 4
  store ptr %p1, ptr %p1.addr, align 8, !tbaa !0
  store i32 %s_p1, ptr %s_p1.addr, align 4, !tbaa !5
  store ptr %p2, ptr %p2.addr, align 8, !tbaa !0
  store i32 %s_p2, ptr %s_p2.addr, align 4, !tbaa !5
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %i_sum) #3
  store i32 0, ptr %i_sum, align 4, !tbaa !5
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %y) #3
  store i32 0, ptr %y, align 4, !tbaa !5
  br label %for.cond

for.cond:                                         ; preds = %for.inc10, %entry
  %0 = load i32, ptr %y, align 4, !tbaa !5
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %0, 16
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %for.cond.cleanup

for.cond.cleanup:                                 ; preds = %for.cond
  store i32 2, ptr %cleanup.dest.slot, align 4
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %y) #3
  br label %for.end12

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.cond
  call void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr %x) #3
  store i32 0, ptr %x, align 4, !tbaa !5
  br label %for.cond1

for.cond1:                                        ; preds = %for.inc, %for.body
  %1 = load i32, ptr %x, align 4, !tbaa !5
  %cmp2 = icmp slt i32 %1, 16
  br i1 %cmp2, label %for.body4, label %for.cond.cleanup3

for.cond.cleanup3:                                ; preds = %for.cond1
  store i32 5, ptr %cleanup.dest.slot, align 4
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %x) #3
  br label %for.end

for.body4:                                        ; preds = %for.cond1
  %2 = load ptr, ptr %p1.addr, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %3 = load i32, ptr %x, align 4, !tbaa !5
  %idxprom = sext i32 %3 to i64
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %2, i64 %idxprom
  %4 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx, align 1, !tbaa !7
  %conv = zext i8 %4 to i32
  %5 = load ptr, ptr %p2.addr, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %6 = load i32, ptr %x, align 4, !tbaa !5
  %idxprom5 = sext i32 %6 to i64
  %arrayidx6 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %5, i64 %idxprom5
  %7 = load i8, ptr %arrayidx6, align 1, !tbaa !7
  %conv7 = zext i8 %7 to i32
  %sub = sub nsw i32 %conv, %conv7
  %8 = call i32 @llvm.abs.i32(i32 %sub, i1 true)
  %9 = load i32, ptr %i_sum, align 4, !tbaa !5
  %add = add nsw i32 %9, %8
  store i32 %add, ptr %i_sum, align 4, !tbaa !5
  br label %for.inc

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %for.body4
  %10 = load i32, ptr %x, align 4, !tbaa !5
  %inc = add nsw i32 %10, 1
  store i32 %inc, ptr %x, align 4, !tbaa !5
  br label %for.cond1, !llvm.loop !8

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.cond.cleanup3
  %11 = load i32, ptr %s_p1.addr, align 4, !tbaa !5
  %12 = load ptr, ptr %p1.addr, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %idx.ext = sext i32 %11 to i64
  %add.ptr = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %12, i64 %idx.ext
  store ptr %add.ptr, ptr %p1.addr, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %13 = load i32, ptr %s_p2.addr, align 4, !tbaa !5
  %14 = load ptr, ptr %p2.addr, align 8, !tbaa !0
  %idx.ext8 = sext i32 %13 to i64
  %add.ptr9 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %14, i64 %idx.ext8
  store ptr %add.ptr9, ptr %p2.addr, align 8, !tbaa !0
  br label %for.inc10

for.inc10:                                        ; preds = %for.end
  %15 = load i32, ptr %y, align 4, !tbaa !5
  %inc11 = add nsw i32 %15, 1
  store i32 %inc11, ptr %y, align 4, !tbaa !5
  br label %for.cond, !llvm.loop !10

for.end12:                                        ; preds = %for.cond.cleanup
  %16 = load i32, ptr %i_sum, align 4, !tbaa !5
  store i32 1, ptr %cleanup.dest.slot, align 4
  call void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr %i_sum) #3
  ret i32 %16
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.start.p0(ptr immarg) #1

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.abs.i32(i32, i1 immarg) #2

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.lifetime.end.p0(ptr immarg) #1

attributes #0 = { nounwind uwtable vscale_range(1,16) "frame-pointer"="non-leaf" "no-trapping-math"="true" "stack-protector-buffer-size"="8" "target-cpu"="generic" "target-features"="+bf16,+bti,+ccidx,+complxnum,+crc,+dit,+dotprod,+ete,+flagm,+fp-armv8,+fp16fml,+fullfp16,+i8mm,+jsconv,+lse,+mte,+neon,+pauth,+perfmon,+predres,+rand,+ras,+rcpc,+rdm,+sb,+spe,+ssbs,+sve,+sve-bitperm,+sve2,+trbe,+v8.1a,+v8.2a,+v8.3a,+v8.4a,+v8.5a,+v8a,+v9a,-fmv" }
attributes #1 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite) }
attributes #2 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }
attributes #3 = { nounwind }

!0 = !{!1, !1, i64 0}
!1 = !{!"p1 omnipotent char", !2, i64 0}
!2 = !{!"any pointer", !3, i64 0}
!3 = !{!"omnipotent char", !4, i64 0}
!4 = !{!"Simple C/C++ TBAA"}
!5 = !{!6, !6, i64 0}
!6 = !{!"int", !3, i64 0}
!7 = !{!3, !3, i64 0}
!8 = distinct !{!8, !9}
!9 = !{!"llvm.loop.mustprogress"}
!10 = distinct !{!10, !9}

define i32 @element_counts_do_not_match_vscale(<vscale x 16 x i32> %v0, <vscale x 8 x i32> %v1) {
  %v0_red = tail call i32 @llvm.vector.reduce.add.nxv16i32(<vscale x 16 x i32> %v0)
  %v1_red = tail call i32 @llvm.vector.reduce.add.nxv8i32(<vscale x 8 x i32> %v1)
  %res = add i32 %v0_red, %v1_red
  ret i32 %res
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.vector.reduce.add.nxv8i32(<vscale x 8 x i32>) #0

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i32 @llvm.vector.reduce.add.nxv16i32(<vscale x 16 x i32>) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

</details>

<details>
<summary>
View the diff from clang-format here.
</summary>

``````````diff
diff --git a/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VectorCombine.cpp b/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VectorCombine.cpp
index 70c7c5a6c..a200abbd5 100644
--- a/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VectorCombine.cpp
+++ b/llvm/lib/Transforms/Vectorize/VectorCombine.cpp
@@ -1813,7 +1813,7 @@ static bool matchAssociativeReduction(
         RHS_Bin = nullptr;
       }
     }
-  } else { // Outer == Sub
+  } else {                      // Outer == Sub
     if (!ScalarReduceCIsLeft) { // (Op0 - C)
       if (InnerOp == Instruction::Add) {
         // (X + Y) - C -> (X - C) + Y
@@ -1873,8 +1873,7 @@ static bool matchAssociativeReduction(
     }
   }
 
-  Value *CombineNode =
-      Builder.CreateBinOp(NewReduceOp, LHS_Reduce, RHS_Reduce);
+  Value *CombineNode = Builder.CreateBinOp(NewReduceOp, LHS_Reduce, RHS_Reduce);
 
   Value *NewBinNode;
   if (LHS_Bin == nullptr)
@@ -1910,8 +1909,8 @@ bool VectorCombine::foldBinopOfReductions(Instruction &I) {
   //   Reduce(Z) - (Reduce(X) + Y)  -> Reduce(Z - X) - Y
   if (auto *Op0 = dyn_cast<BinaryOperator>(I.getOperand(0))) {
     if (matchAssociativeReduction(I, BinOpOpc, ReductionIID, Builder, Op0,
-                                  I.getOperand(1), /*ScalarReduceCIsLeft=*/false,
-                                  ReplaceValue))
+                                  I.getOperand(1),
+                                  /*ScalarReduceCIsLeft=*/false, ReplaceValue))
       return true;
   }
 
@@ -1922,8 +1921,6 @@ bool VectorCombine::foldBinopOfReductions(Instruction &I) {
       return true;
   }
 
-
-
   Value *V0 = checkIntrinsicAndGetItsArgument(I.getOperand(0), ReductionIID);
   if (!V0)
     return false;

``````````

comments.author: davemgreen
comments.body: When is this expected to be better? Some architectures have efficient instructions for add(vecreduce), and this change might just end up messing that up.

comments.author: Anjian-Wen
comments.body:
> Shouldn't this be handed in reassociation? VectorCombine is primarily a cost driven optimisation pass

Thanks for your review! This patch tries to reassociate the vector reduce_add Node in some pattern only for folding two reduce_add into one in the later foldBinopOfReductions function in VectorCombine Pass, I think maybe we don't need to reassociate that early in Reassociation Pass? And I think maybe it is easier to understand to put the change here?

comments.author: Anjian-Wen
comments.body:
> When is this expected to be better? Some architectures have efficient instructions for add(vecreduce), and this change might just end up messing that up.

Thanks for your review! like the .ll test, this patch supposed to change the order of the pattern like `(ReduceX - Y) + ReduceC -> (ReduceX + ReduceC) - Y`, and give it a chance for foldBinopOfReductions to decide whether to fold. I don't know if I understand it well, If you mean add two vecreduce, this patch won't change that kind of pattern. If not, could you help give any example .ll?
